question: How many DORs were created in Q4 2025?
SQL: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM requests WHERE created_at BETWEEN '2025-10-01' AND '2026-01-01';

question: Who is blocking the progress for DOR #50610?
evidence: People blocking progress are those assigned to tasks with status 'in_progress' - includes both PICs and collaborators
SQL: SELECT name FROM (SELECT users.name FROM request_tasks JOIN users ON request_tasks.pic_id = users.id WHERE request_tasks.request_id = 50610 AND request_tasks.status = 'in_progress' UNION SELECT users.name FROM request_tasks JOIN request_task_collaborators ON request_task_collaborators.request_task_id = request_tasks.id JOIN users ON request_task_collaborators.user_id = users.id WHERE request_tasks.request_id = 50610 AND request_tasks.status = 'in_progress');
answer: Bhavit Mehta and Dipayan Majumder are blocking the progress for DOR #50610.

question: Give me all of the DOR IDs for BOM 699-5G147-0520-RC2
evidence: DOR ID is the request_id in request_boms table
SQL: SELECT request_id FROM request_boms JOIN boms ON boms.id = request_boms.bom_id WHERE boms.name = '699-5G147-0520-RC2';

question: What is the most frequently used GPU MODS version?
evidence: GPU MODS version is stored in request_task_fields with field_name = 'modsVersion'
SQL: SELECT text_value FROM request_attribute_values WHERE field_name = 'modsVersion' GROUP BY text_value ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
answer: From the request_attribute_values table (field modsVersion), the most frequently used GPU MODS version is: 570.384.

question: What is the most frequently used GPU MODS version?
SQL: SELECT request_id FROM request_boms JOIN boms ON boms.id = request_boms.bom_id WHERE boms.name = '699-5G147-0520-RC2';

question: What are three most frequently occurring processes across all requests?
evidence: Process frequency is counted through request_processes join table
SQL: SELECT processes.name, COUNT(*) FROM processes JOIN request_processes ON request_processes.process_id = processes.id GROUP BY processes.id ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 3;
answer: The three most frequently occurring processes are FLA (901 requests), FCT (849), and IOT (376).

question: What is the average size of all user provided request attachments, in KB?
evidence: 1 KB = 1024 bytes; size is stored in size_bytes column
SQL: SELECT AVG(size_bytes) / 1024 AS kilobytes FROM request_comment_attachments;
answer: The average size of user-provided request attachments is about 245 KB.

question: Please present me with an ordered list of all tasks by their rollback frequency, in descending order
evidence: Rollback frequency is counted by filtering request_task_events where event_type = 'rollback'
SQL: SELECT tasks.name, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE request_task_events.event_type = 'rollback') FROM tasks JOIN workflow_tasks ON workflow_tasks.task_id = tasks.id JOIN filter_rule_group_tasks ON filter_rule_group_tasks.workflow_task_id = workflow_tasks.id JOIN request_tasks ON request_tasks.filter_rule_group_task_id = filter_rule_group_tasks.id JOIN request_task_events ON request_task_events.request_task_id = request_tasks.id GROUP BY tasks.id ORDER BY 2 DESC;
answer_raw:
| name                                  |   count |
|:--------------------------------------|--------:|
| Test Package                          |     401 |
| BOM Coverage                          |     226 |
| Relevant Entities                     |     185 |
| Test Station Results                  |     108 |
| GPU MODS                              |      67 |
| Nautilus                              |      61 |
| Validation Station Results            |      55 |
| Firmware Recipe Bug                   |      52 |
| Supplemental Files                    |      41 |
| CMs                                   |      40 |
| Factory Acceptance Criteria Bug       |      31 |
| OneDiag                               |      26 |
| Log Review                            |      16 |
| Golden Template Files                 |      14 |
| Diag Review                           |      13 |
| External Test Package                 |      13 |
| PDP                                   |      12 |
| Datacenter Fields                     |      12 |
| Firmware Review                       |      10 |
| Display Driver                        |       8 |
| Software Review                       |       8 |
| Tegra MODS                            |       6 |
| Source of Truth                       |       4 |
| Close Factory Acceptance Criteria Bug |       3 |
| Factory                               |       2 |
| ECO                                   |       1 |
| Heat Sink                             |       1 |
| SharePoint                            |       1 |
| Close Firmware Recipe Bug             |       0 |
| SFTP                                  |       0 |
| NVTask                                |       0 |
| SLT Build Config                      |       0 |
| PDK                                   |       0 |
answer: Tasks by rollback frequency (highest first): Test Package (401), BOM Coverage (226), Relevant Entities (185), Test Station Results (108), GPU MODS (67), Nautilus (61), Validation Station Results (55), Firmware Recipe Bug (52), Supplemental Files (41), CMs (40), and 21 more tasks with lower counts down to PDK (0).

question: What percentage of all tasks have at least one collaborator?
evidence: Calculate as (count of tasks with collaborators / total count of tasks) * 100
SQL: SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT request_tasks.id) FILTER (WHERE request_task_collaborators.id IS NOT NULL) / COUNT(DISTINCT request_tasks.id) AS percentage FROM request_tasks LEFT JOIN request_task_collaborators ON request_task_collaborators.request_task_id = request_tasks.id;
answer: About 7.6% of all tasks have at least one collaborator.

question: Which Pcodes appear in multiple business units?
evidence: Pcode belongs to multiple BUs when the distinct count of bus.id is greater than 1
SQL: SELECT pcodes.name, STRING_AGG(DISTINCT bus.name, ', ' ORDER BY bus.name) AS business_units FROM pcodes JOIN products ON products.id = pcodes.product_id JOIN bus ON bus.id = products.bu_id GROUP BY pcodes.name HAVING COUNT (DISTINCT bus.id) > 1;
answer_raw:
| name   | business_units   |
|:-------|:-----------------|
| PG147  | GPU, Tesla       |
| PG153  | GPU, Tesla       |
answer: Two Pcodes appear in multiple business units: PG147 (GPU, Tesla) and PG153 (GPU, Tesla).

question: What are the top 10 most frequently occurring trainstop and quality level combinations?
evidence: Count combinations by grouping on both quality_levels.id and trainstops.id
SQL: SELECT quality_levels.name, trainstops.name, COUNT(*) FROM requests JOIN quality_levels ON requests.quality_level_id = quality_levels.id JOIN trainstops ON requests.trainstop_id = trainstops.id GROUP BY quality_levels.id, trainstops.id ORDER BY 3 DESC LIMIT 10;
answer: The top 10 trainstop and quality level combinations by frequency are: MP (303), CR (169), TS1 (108), TS2 (83), CR2 (55), TS1 (47), MP (46), MP (45), TS2 (41), and EB1 (41).
